answers.field_label: Are You Under A Physicians Care Now
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: If Yes Please Explain Under Physicians Care
answers.value: Currently followed by my primary care physician, Dr. Laura Chen (Internal Medicine, Pacific Primary Care in Seattle), for mild generalized anxiety disorder and seasonal allergies. I see her once a year for an annual physical and as needed if something comes up. I am taking sertraline 50 mg once daily for anxiety, which has been stable for about 3 years with good effect and no significant side effects other than some initial mild nausea that resolved. For allergies, I use over-the-counter cetirizine 10 mg as needed during spring and early summer when pollen counts are high, and I have a prescription for fluticasone nasal spray that I use intermittently when my congestion is bad (usually 1–2 sprays in each nostril once daily during peak allergy season). I also see a dermatologist once a year for a skin check because of my fair skin and a history of a mildly atypical mole removed in 2019, but there has been no skin cancer diagnosed. Overall, my conditions are well controlled and I am generally healthy.

answers.field_label: Have You Ever Been Hospitalized Or Had A Major Operation
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: If Yes Please Explain Hospitalized Or Major Operation
answers.value: Yes. 1) Appendectomy in 2010: I was hospitalized for acute appendicitis at Swedish Medical Center in Seattle in June 2010 when I was 23. I had a laparoscopic appendectomy under general anesthesia, stayed overnight for observation, and was discharged the next day. Recovery was uneventful, and I have had no complications since then. 2) Childbirth in 2018: I was admitted to the University of Washington Medical Center in October 2018 for the birth of my daughter. It was a vaginal delivery with an epidural. I stayed in the hospital for two nights postpartum. I had a second-degree perineal tear that was repaired at the time of delivery and healed normally. No significant complications for me or the baby. Other than those two events, I have not had any other major surgeries or hospital stays.

answers.value: Allergic to penicillin-class antibiotics: I developed a diffuse, itchy, red rash and hives over my trunk and arms along with mild facial swelling after taking amoxicillin for a sinus infection in college (around 2006). I went to urgent care, where they told me to stop the medication immediately and treated me with oral antihistamines and a short course of steroids. I did not have trouble breathing or require hospitalization, but I was advised to avoid penicillin and related antibiotics in the future. Since then, I have always informed medical and dental providers about this allergy, and they have used non-penicillin alternatives when antibiotics were needed. No known allergies to foods, latex, metals, local anesthetics, or other medications beyond this penicillin reaction.
